name: Time Traveller
content: I travel in time; I am a time traveller.
Men in the earliest sunrise of the century would arrive before they departed. They were supersonic.
I start at sunrise and finish at night; I do not see light. I am a time traveller.
Plastics and composite metals—and, if we're feeling fancy, glass—convolute into warp.
Often there is paper to accompany them, but sometimes not just for efficiency's sake.
I wear a suit and tie if I want them to smile at my chair, and perhaps some shorts if I want them to kneel.
Pupils stoop to my level: “Which class, sir?”
